title: Secure Web Application Project: Authentication, RBAC, and Best Practices
hardestPart: This project was difficult due to needing to build a complex full stack security backend, without having been taught about full stack architecture. My school curriculum was unfortunately not very organized and my class missed out on learning key security patterns, data structures, and code management methods.
architecture: Client-Server REST API following a modular MVC (Model-View-Controller) pattern
perfSecurity:
This project has:
- Authentication
- Role Based Authorization Control(RBAC)
- Session Management
- Data Protection/Validation
- Vulnerability Mitigation
You can read more in the repository documentation.
lessonsLearned: Building this project taught me that security has significantly more complexity than I expected. It’s one thing to hash a password with Bcrypt, but it’s a much bigger challenge to sync JWTs and CSRF protection without ruining the user experience. I realized that implementing invisible security is difficult, and that properly identifying the seams at which different utilities interact is important in order to optimize for UX.
futureImprovements: Next time, I’d add Refresh Tokens so users don't get logged out every 15 minutes and implement Blind Indexing to make searching through encrypted database fields much faster.
prerequisites: Node.js (v14 or higher), npm, MongoDB, Git
installCommand:
npm install 
 mkdir certs 
 openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:2048 -keyout certs/key.pem -out certs/cert.pem -sha256 -days 365 -nodes -subj "/CN=localhost"
